summary: First-iteration full-stack test of Ordia. Backend: 16/16 pytest tests pass, covering auth (register+seed catalog, login demo, wrong-password 401, /me guard, duplicate-email 400), catalog (multi-tenant isolation + CRUD), AI extraction via text/CSV/image (Claude Sonnet 4.6 via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY), order lifecycle (list/get/update/validate), export CSV+JSON (correct mime + content-disposition + status->exported), and dashboard stats. Frontend E2E: demo login -> Dashboard 'Centro di Comando' -> Nuovo Ordine via new-order-cta -> use-sample-order -> extract-order-button -> OrderReview (5 line items with match-select components + order-source visible) -> validate-order-button (green toast 'Ordine validato') -> nav-orders -> nav-catalog with working search. No blocking errors surfaced on the pages.

issues: Console React hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option>. Caused by Emergent dev tooling wrapping option text with an x-file-name span; no user-visible impact. Not an app-code bug — can be ignored in preview builds.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py packs auth + catalog + extraction + orders + dashboard into one 550-line file. Approaching the 700-line ceiling; consider splitting into routers/auth.py, routers/orders.py, services/extraction.py before growing further.

Login lockout keys on ip+email but the same key is reused when a valid password comes in AFTER a lockout was set; delete happens on success (good). However, wrong-password paths always extend locked_until by 15 min on every failure (rolling window), which is arguably desired but should be documented.
CORS currently defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True which browsers actually ignore — fine for Bearer-token flow used here, but flag if cookie auth is ever added.

/api/orders/extract catches invalid JSON from the LLM by falling back to substring{...} parse; if the LLM returns text with no braces the order is saved with empty line_items and status='ready'. Consider returning a 502 in that case instead of a silent empty order.
Product update endpoint does not return 404 if company_id mismatches after matched_count==0 — currently handled correctly via composite filter. OK.
Dashboard.js does .catch(() => {}) silently on /dashboard/stats failure — user sees skeletons forever if the API errors. Show an error state.
Login.js hardcodes a demo credential hint in the UI ('demo@ordia.app / demo123'). Fine for demo, remove for production.
test_credentials: Demo: demo@ordia.app / demo123. Newly registered test companies use test_<uuid>@example.com / testpass123.
seed_data_creation: Each test module registers ONE new company (test_<uuid>@example.com) and creates one seeded order via /api/orders/extract for downstream Orders/Dashboard classes. Under xdist loadscope with 2 workers, each worker registers its own company. Products created by CRUD test are cleaned up; seeded order and its parent company remain (multi-tenant, no cross-contamination).
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py already migrated to pytest with xdist loadscope. Module-scoped `new_company` and `seeded_order` fixtures give each worker its own tenant + order so extraction results are available for both Orders and Dashboard suites. If image extraction test ever flakes on LLM latency, timeout is set to 120s. To rerun: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/backend_test.py -v`. Frontend flow verified end-to-end using demo account. Ordia UI is Italian — rely on data-testid, not text.
rca of the issue: No app bugs. Two test-file issues were fixed during this iteration: (1) initial register used @ordia.test which pydantic EmailStr rejects as reserved TLD — switched to @example.com; (2) TestOrders/TestDashboard originally relied on TestExtraction to seed an order but xdist loadscope splits classes across workers so the state didn't cross workers — introduced module-scoped seeded_order fixture that each worker builds independently.
